rationales.hook_strength: 'Still wiped out' after drinking water is a relatable, specific frustration that stops the scroll — loses one point because the electrolyte angle is now common enough that savvy viewers may swipe before the sub-copy lands.
rationales.clarity: Each slide is a single, named idea with a one-sentence payoff — the mechanism, signs, who's at risk, fix, and limit are all distinct and actionable.
rationales.audience_fit: 'Afternoon headaches, fog, tired by 3pm' maps exactly onto the busy 20–35 audience who already drink water but can't figure out why they still feel off.
rationales.novelty: Electrolytes-aren't-just-water is a well-traveled TikTok angle; the 'pinch of salt' fix and low-carb callout add some texture but won't feel new to anyone who's been in wellness content for six months.
rationales.brand_fit: Plain language throughout, claims are hedged ('classic signs,' 'covers most people'), no banned words, and the CTA ties the feature to a real behavior the app actually tracks.
rationales.predicted_performance: Solid saves from people finally naming their afternoon crash, but the saturated electrolyte category caps its shareability ceiling — expect average, not breakout, relative to a typical health carousel.
